verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted protein from the cysteine-rich secretory protein family, expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal fluid. While primarily soluble, CRISP1 is known to bind to sperm plasma membranes during epididymal transit and remains stably associated with sperm cells as they move through the reproductive tract. This stable non-covalent or wash-resistant association with sperm surface proteins (particularly through interactions with ion channels and other sperm-surface receptors) constitutes a documented mechanism of surface attachment in the specialized context of the male reproductive tract. The protein thus reaches the cell surface via a stable post-secretory mechanism in its physiological niche.
